title: Ipomoea hispida
intro: Ipomoea hispida, also known as the hairy morning glory, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Ipomoea hispida include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Ipomoea hispida is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, and its extracts are also used in capsules or tinctures to leverage its therapeutic properties.
taxonomy: Ipomoea hispida is a species of flowering plant in the morning glory family (Convolvulaceae). It is native to tropical Asia and is also known as the fishbone cactus. The plant is a perennial vine with hairy stems and leaves. Ipomoea hispida is classified as a member of the genus Ipomoea and the family Convolvulaceae. This classification reflects its evolutionary relationship with other plants in the morning glory family.
